content.full_text:
CITY OF MERCED
O.:tober 22, 1-990
Ma.i'k- iolc;r:odomi, Esquire
F. P. P. C.
P. C. P,c>: 8.07
sacramento, cA 95812
Re: F.equest for Letter of Opinion
Dear Mr. Morodomi:
This l-etter is to folIow up on our telephone conversation
durring which you concluded, based upon the facts presented,
that Mercedrs Mayor, Bruce Gabriault has a potential
conflict of interest regarding the decision to go forward
with a proposed Civic Center/Hote1 project. 
The relevant
facts are restated herein.
The Mayor owns a T.V./Appliance business and the real
property where the business operates. The property is
approximately 1700 feet from the proposed project. 
The
business is governed by subsection (g) of Section L8702.2 of
your regulations, and the real property is governed by
Section (a) (3) of your regulation 18702.3.
The proposed project would take ten to twenty million
dollars to build and could involve a civic center/senior
center, multi-story public parking garage, and a privately
owned hotel. 
It is contemplated that the project would be
financed by Redevelopment tax increment bonds. The City
Council acts as the Redevelopment Board.
The project is located in the 'fdowntown'r central core. Only
a small percentage of the City limits is }ocated within 2500
feet of the proposed project, and approximately 15% of the
City's businesses are located with a 2500 foot radius. 
The
errtire redevelopment project area contains about 25% of the
Ci.ty' s businesses .
678 West lBth Street . 
Merced, California 95340


Pfease eonfirm that based upon these facts, it was your
conclusion that the decision on the proposed project could
have material financial impact on the Mayorrs business and
real property and that he should not participate in any
decision to move forward on the project.
Very trul-y yours,
SEN : mt
steven- F. NoYd
2
Cit-y Attorney
